patch.value: pm health ok:false from legacy unused 'index' required subdir

pm health reported ok:false on a fully-healthy repo solely because of warning missing_directory:index. The 'index' subdir was in PM_CORE_REQUIRED_SUBDIRS since the original bootstrap commit but is never written or read — search keyword artifacts live in search/, and grep confirms no code path uses an index/ dir. It is a legacy search-artifact location that gc still cleans for old repos. Removed 'index' from PM_CORE_REQUIRED_SUBDIRS so init no longer creates an empty unused dir and health no longer false-warns. gc still cleans legacy index/ for old repos. Updated the constants assertion test and the gc/integration tests that seeded index/ (they now mkdir it explicitly to test legacy cleanup).

Removed 'index' from PM_CORE_REQUIRED_SUBDIRS (src/core/shared/constants.ts). pm health now reports ok:true on the dev repo. gc still cleans legacy index/ for old repos (unchanged). Updated shared-constants-errors.spec PM_REQUIRED_SUBDIRS assertion and the gc + cli.integration tests that seeded index/manifest.json (now mkdir the dir explicitly). Tests green.
